headline: AI Weekly: sneakers and SpaceX fuel AI investment frenzy
summary:
STORY: From sneakers to SpaceX... This is AI Weekly.

:: AI Weekly

Another AI company is off and running... and, this time, it's coming from former sneaker brand Allbirds.

Allbirds officially changed its name to Smartbird, cementing its months-long transformation from shoemaker to AI infrastructure firm.

And sending its shares up more than 30%.

The move comes after the company in April said it was shifting its focus to offer cloud computing capacity...

and AI services, leading to a more than five-fold jump in its shares. 

The newly-named Smartbird also announced the appointment of former Amazon executive Nadia Carlsten as its new CEO.

G7 leaders discussed artificial intelligence over lunch, including the liability of bots and agents...

and how AI presents truth and falsehood, on the last day of a summit of the Group of Seven.

OpenAI founder Sam Altman and Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei attended the meeting in the lakeside resort town in France. 

U.S. President Donald Trump said negotiations with artificial intelligence company Anthropic are, quote, "going fine."

Trump made the comment to reporters at the G7 summit after the meeting between leaders and tech bosses.

Anthropic is in a dispute with the Trump administration over foreign access to its most advanced AI models.

The company had disabled access for all users to those models after Trump ordered Anthropic to block foreign ​nationals from accessing them.

Chinese AI startup DeepSeek has avoided being added to a U.S. trade blacklist.

Sources have told Reuters that Washington has held off updating the so-called Entity List.

DeepSeek, and more than 100 other Chinese companies including chipmaker CXMT, had been approved for addition to the list last year by an interagency committee.

That’s according to exclusive new reporting by Reuters.

DeepSeek has been in Washington’s crosshairs for some time.

Last year, the U.S. State Department said the firm was aiding Beijing’s military and intelligence operations.

Shares of rocket maker SpaceX soared as much as 17% this weeek...

catapulting the company's market value past Amazon's and briefly above Microsoft's.

That makes SpaceX the fifth most valuable company in the world... just days after its blockbuster public debut.

If the gains hold it will give SpaceX a market cap of around $2.6 trillion. 

Investors piled into the world's largest IPO and bet on Elon Musk's sprawling empire spanning rockets, internet service and AI.

Anna Rathbun is founder and CEO of Grenadilla Advisory.

"When the multiples go up, they want to IPO. And when multiples are up, we're all very excited. So I think it's not so much about SpaceX, but just the excitement about AI and SpaceX, as much as the word has space in it, it's an AI company at the end of the day,"

headline: Amazon explores selling AI chips to challenge Nvidia
summary: Investing.com -- Amazon is in talks to sell its Trainium AI chips directly to other companies for use in their data centers, according to a report from Bloomberg News, citing an interview with Amazon’s AI Chief, Peter DeSantis. The move represents an effort to compete with Nvidia and take advantage of growing global demand for AI infrastructure.

The move represents an effort to compete with Nvidia and take advantage of growing global demand for AI infrastructure.

headline: Waymo Has a Tesla-Like Recall. What It Means for Alphabet Stock.

The  Alphabet  -owned ride-hailing network operator is expanding across the country, along with other companies, including  Tesla Amazon.com  -backed Zoox, and others.

headline: Architect Labs raises $24 million to take on Broadcom, Marvell custom chip business
summary: Architect Labs said on Thursday it had raised $24 million in seed funding to ‌build a company that will use artificial intelligence to speed and ‌ease the design of custom chips.  The custom chips designed by Marvell and Broadcom generate tens of billions of dollars in revenue and offer an alternative to ‌Nvidia's powerful hardware.  Architect Labs aims ⁠to cheapen and speed the process, which now takes roughly two years and costs hundreds of millions of dollars ⁠in labor costs and research and development.

headline: Amazon (AMZN) Partners With Odyssey ML To Push Trainium Into World Model AI
summary: Amazon.com (NasdaqGS:AMZN) has partnered with Odyssey ML, a world model AI company founded by self-driving veterans. Odyssey ML plans to use Amazon Web Services as its primary cloud provider for training and running its advanced AI systems. The collaboration includes optimizing Odyssey ML's models for Amazon’s in-house AWS Trainium chips. The partnership focuses on world model AI with potential uses in robotics, gaming, simulation, and scientific applications. For Amazon.com, this...

headline: Should Amazon’s US$10 Billion Missouri AI Bet and Trainium Chip Sales Shift AMZN’s Cloud Narrative?

headline: PLTR Stock: The Math Hidden In Its Price
summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock trades at $130.63 per share, a market cap of $312.7B, and 137.1x trailing P/E. That is a meaningful premium. The question is not whether the stock is "cheap" or "expensive" in the abstract. It is what the market is implicitly assuming about the business in order to justify paying this price.

content.title: PLTR Stock: The Math Hidden In Its Price
content.summary: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) stock trades at $130.63 per share, a market cap of $312.7B, and 137.1x trailing P/E. That is a meaningful premium. The question is not whether the stock is "cheap" or "expensive" in the abstract. It is what the market is implicitly assuming about the business in order to justify paying this price.
